You're not the only one who has lied to me. When I was yelling at Meng Tieyi earlier, that was all for you, too.
Right, I forgot to tell you this. Honestly, I think it is impressive for someone to travel so far and work so hard to make her living.
Now's not the time for—
Remember this. If you don't make it out, Yumen won't make it through the Catastrophe either.
This is a tall order, but Xingyu Logistics accepts.
Close your eyes.
Lin Yühsia shakes her hands, and the glass shards fall to the ground, instantly turning to quicksand.
Streams of fine sand flow from the corners and ceiling, quickly converging as one.
Still feel like putting up a—
The sand rises.
In the cramped warehouse, a sandstorm forms.
Did something catch fire over there?
Could it be arson? Let's go take a look.
The sand sluice?
Who would even come here?
True.
The military whistle sounds from the top of the city. The sky is already pitch black, and the outline of the full moons can be vaguely seen through the clouds.
If the old smith has finished his work and returns to the Sword Forge to find her missing, he could probably figure out that he should look for her over at the sand sluice.
The nomadic city foundations chew through the ground, the high-speed turbines grinding up oversized obstacles along with tons of sand and gravel, spitting them out with a deafening sound.
The old smith was right, there are no guards posted here, because no one would ever want to leave the city this way.
(It's almost time...)
(Violent braying)
Three times you've gotten away from me. And lucky enough, you've once again walked yourself into a dead end.
I don't know you.
The sword on your back has nothing to do with our plans, but an object hiding one-twelfth of the bestial Sui's consciousness is of great interest to someone.
Bestial Sui? What are you talking about...?
Sounds like you don't know anything. That makes you even less worthy of holding that sword.
Hand it over.
A black halo behind your head... an Anasa?
So what if I am...?
An Anasa actively mingling in a Yanese nomadic city? How ridiculous.
Have you forgotten where you came from? Forgotten what happened to your people? While you were suffering out in the wilderness, aimlessly looking for food and shelter, did Yan ever help you?
Anasa, you should join the Shanhaizhong organization.
All I see is the terrible things you've done in the city.
Then you're a fool.
(The sand will soon disperse.)
(There's no way I could match the woman Chief Wei and Dad couldn't beat together.)
(But I can at least delay her for a while longer, to buy Du Yaoye a little more time to make her way out...)
You're scared of me, but you gave another the opportunity to escape.
It seems that after all these years, you people have not changed a bit. Consider it an honor to die a brave death.
Gallantry isn't exactly my style, and I'm not about to let you kill me.
A crunch sounds, and within the next second the yellow sand condenses into a glass blade.
Trying a sneak attack?
You lot really enjoy your cheap tricks.
To try and sneak an attack while ignoring the disparity in strength is just asking to die.
In the end, so many 'means' were employed to force Meng Tieyi out, and obtain the Catastrophe data.
I guess I could count this... as having done a good deed?
Oh well.
The glass sword crumbles inch by inch, the long blade already falling on her brow.

lines.st: Chi Xiao, Rouse Qi—
A crimson sword tears through the airborne sand and dust.

lines.x: Swords clash, the sound rings through the air wrought by friction. Lin Yühsia's sword shifts its form between sand and glass, countless shards in the air reflecting a silhouette within.
The figure stands before her, tall and straight as a poplar.
How did you know I would be here?
What are you talking about?
I saw your Arts in the distance, I figured you were asking me to come save you.

Don't flatter yourself, I had no idea you were coming to Yumen—
So why are you here?
Just passing through.
Did you follow me here, like you did in Dossoles?
Wasn't that you following me?!
You must've heard about the assassination attempt on Wei?
If you've got time to talk at me, you've got time to explain. What exactly is going on here?
The sand and dust have dissipated completely. Not a soul remains in the warehouse.
I don't have time, we have to get to the city gates!
Du Yaoye has been in the city for a while, but she never realized the streets went on for so long.
Just a hundred meters feels more difficult than traversing the mountain roads of Xunri Peak. The small box in her arms feels heavier than any quarry shouldered by the mountain pole-carriers.

Be it the Escort Association or a logistics company, things will always be the same. For the sake of our livelihood, we put our lives on the line. If you wish to make a name for yourself in this business, you need to understand how heavy the weight on your shoulders is.
I'll never forget all the stories about escort missions Dad would tell me, even if he kept repeating them over and over again. Or maybe because he did.
The principle is the same every time, but you only really understand it after experiencing it for yourself.
(Faster, faster...)
(I can see the gates!)
They've almost caught up to me?!
Du Yaoye is skilled in the art of using the wind to anticipate danger. If she focuses without distraction, she could absolutely dodge this arrow.
The arrow pierces her right shoulder, causing the box to slip out of her grasp. She hastily reaches out with her left hand to catch it, but ends up falling to the ground, along with the box.
Silence her and retrieve the object.
Miss Du!
It's you...
I was chasing another group of bad guys and ended up here.
Last night, when I hurried over to the Sword Forge, I saw the mandarins taking you away with them... What's going on here...?
Don't wanna talk about it...
Help me out, let's take care of these guys first.
Good plan.
Loose your arrows.
Impudence.
A stretch of silk, a patch of snow, or perhaps the gleaming of a sword.
The arrows fall to the ground, neatly sliced in two.
You're... the one who saved Zuo Le last night...
Help me. I have something very important to deliver to the Lord Exorcist.
He's at the outer city tower, and you're seriously wounded. Can you still run?
Save the goods, then the men.
Very well, then leave them to us.
Right, that Envoy Lin of yours is still holed up in the warehouse fighting some evil woman...
Go help her.
Yühsia!
Right. You get moving.
Again with public acts of violence. You Shanhaizhong have gone too far.
Master.
Where's the girl?
I almost got her, but these two stopped me.
Useless.
Hurry after her and get the data.
You shall not pass.
You think you can stop me? Let's see if you can dodge my blade another time.
So she's the evil lady Du was talking about?
If she's here, then that means Yühsia is...
I'm fine.
Finally caught up.
Yühsia... is that... Superintendent Ch'en?!
That's her alright. Where's Du Yaoye?
She's already on the move.
Good. Then there's only one thing left to do here.
Stop that woman.
Boring.
It seems that after all these years, you people have not changed a bit. You don't know how to respect power.
A colony of ants, is still just ants.
The wind blows away the clouds, and moonlight shines on the long and empty street.
The road is clean, save for a few yellow leaves swirling around the stone bricks.
It's the kind of cleanliness that comes after the decline of prosperity.
Like a bleak autumn night.
It all ends here.
Autumn night...?
So she's behind all the chaos that's been plaguing the city?
It's time to put an end to her troublemaking.
Be careful. Her Arts are extremely strange.
Even my father and Chief Wei working together couldn't capture her. She's got a lot of tricks up her sleeve.
You're telling me that this woman, talking all that trash, is the one who tried to assassinate Wei Yenwu?
You scared?
You've got to be kidding me.
Sounds perfect. We'll knock her out right here and I'll get to shove it in Wei Yenwu's face.
Alright.
Let's settle this here and now.
Someone! Anyone!
You again! Didn't we just let you out?
Keep making noise, and it's no more Mr. Nice Guy!
Daizi!
This box is holding something that can save the city, so shut up and give it to Zuo.
This is... data for the Imperial Astronomical Bureau? And a token?
Girl? How did this come into your possession? Hey, wake up!
Hurry! Call the medic!
Where... where exactly did this woman come from?
Has Superintendent Ch'en finally met her match?
Chi Xiao can't cut through her Arts.
If you can even call it Arts in the first place...
She doesn't seem to know any kung fu, but her swordplay is ridiculously quick.
It's like the kind of kung fu you find in those fake 'forbidden techniques' manuals that say they'll teach you transcend space and time.
I'll trade a few more blows with her and see if I can figure it out...
Careful now, she's not playing with us.
Slip up once and you might die.
Even if you don't, you still will.
You want a few more rounds with me? Sure, let's start with you.
This time, her blade flies quicker than in their previous bouts. Waai Fu can only feel the autumn wind brushing against her face, leaving her no time to react.
Suddenly, a wall appears in front of her, or rather, a man's back as thick and wide as a wall.
Waai Fu looks at the wall in front of her that she last saw over a decade ago. Still, the sight overlaps with the one in her memories.
The reunion comes sooner than she expected, and catches her off guard.
So this is the one Lee mentioned, the one who's been bothering everyone?
You again?
Turns out I've been sleeping bad because I was about to run into someone I'd rather not see.
Huai Tianpei sweeps a quick glance behind him.
His movements are light and quick, but Waai Fu catches them perfectly clearly.
I didn't want to fight anybody until my match with the Grandmaster.
*sigh*... Guess today's a cheat day.
So yeah, you've got some skills, but I can't say I know any kung fu school where you bully your juniors for practice.
If you're itching for a fight, I'll take you on!
Huai, you're at a disadvantage unarmed. I'll lend you my sword.
Don't need it.
Anything my fists can't break, a sword won't cut either.
Don't overestimate yourself.
Haah—!
Waai Fu gets a front row seat to the action.
Sidestep, chop, quick step, twist, elbow strike. All moves she has learned herself.
Seeing the pained expression on his opponent's face, it is clear that Huai Tianpei is using his full strength.
Waai Fu imagines what it feels like to be slammed into a wall.
Right...
That's some blade. I dodged it by a hair, but it caught my skin.
Damn you—

After being knocked back several feet, the woman steadies herself with her blade. The tremendous impact seems to have little lasting effect on her, as she lifts her sword again and assumes a defensive stance.
But being knocked off-balance has made her furious, and anger whips into a tangible wind, with everyone present feeling a chill run up their spines at the same time.
The moonlight intensifies.
Still got more tricks under her sleeve?
This sensation again...
Oh? Her Qi feels different... Great, let's go for another round!
(low-pitched hissing)
The woman raises her head and glances toward the sky, the clouds drifting across and obscuring half the moonlight.
She lowers her half-raised blade.
Since the girl already delivered the data, I'm not going bother with you any longer.
It doesn't matter. You're out of time.
Yumen city won't escape this calamity.
Seems like we weren't needed.
We did witness a splendid showdown, though, so I wouldn't say it was a waste.
The young will surpass us in time.
I assumed you were referring to that fellow.
I'd say Ch'en here has already learned a good chunk of what you have to offer.
What do you think of her swordplay?
Hmm...
Skillful, but not yet the peak of perfection.
Oh?
So what you mean to say is...
Brother Meng—!
A sorrowful howl resounds through the long and deserted street.
Back at the agency, Waai Fu saw plenty of soap operas depicting children reunited with their parents.
Some cried bitterly while others threw furniture around, the actors' intense performances felt strange and unfamiliar to Waai Fu.
When watching with Hung or Lee, they would always silently change the channel.

She tried to imagine what it would be like when she saw him again. The pent-up resentment, anger, confusion, and dissatisfaction that accumulated over a decade... How would she express herself to him?
But at this very moment, the man standing right in front of her feels less real than the cold wind blowing on her face.
You came to my rescue, so thanks for that.
Sure...
Anything else you'd like to say?
I'm good.
Then prepare yourself.
Alright.
Dad, what does 'Hung Mei Wing Chun' mean?
Of all the countless kung fu styles under Heaven, you choose this one, huh? Alright, I'll teach you this one.
Which style do you use, dad? I want to learn the same one as you.
I'll teach you mine, but not now. You still haven't mastered the basics. Once you've got a solid foundation, then you can learn my kung fu.
Okay!
I've sparred with him so many times that I've lost count.
The moment our fists connect, my arm adapts with my muscle memory. More submerged memories also flow out from my mind.
A small courtyard with blue brick and stone flooring, a large porcelain water jar always filled full, wooden stakes wrapped with cloth... and a tall figure whose face could never be clearly seen.
That was my world, day after day, diligently practicing and enduring hardships, year after year, cultivating my mind and tempering my character.
My sparring partner changed from my father to a wooden dummy, never letting myself rest, even for a day. Over the past decade, practicing kung fu became as natural as eating and sleeping.
I think I asked him the question.
'Why?'
Why did you teach me kung fu, why did you have me become a warrior just like you?
The intertwining of moves and techniques, the simple and genuine connection.
You've taught me so much.
But I was never chasing you.
I've found my own goals, I know why I practice kung fu, and I won't change the path I chose just because a man's back popped up in front of me.
And I will surpass you.
A fist lands on the man's robust chest, as if hitting a thick wall.
It gives off a muffled sound.
You've improved over the years, but you're still a long way from matching me.
Big talk. But I wouldn't have even landed a hit on you back then.
If this is as far as you've come in a dozen years, then I'll catch up to you real soon.
My daughter enjoys some big talk.
That's not a compliment.
I lose.
Huai Tianpei twirls his beard, thinks for a long time, and suddenly bursts into laughter.
That move where you kicked me in the chin doesn't look like anything I taught you.
...What fun.
Anyway, I'm heading back to the clinic.
Huai, wait a second.
Do you need me for something?
Where is the Grandmaster's sword? And Zuo Le?
The sword's with the girl, and that mandarin boy probably went after her.
Can't say for sure. I'm pretty much done with all this stuff.
Wait.
The Grandmaster wants to see you.
Oh?
I've been going easy on you, since you're Anasa.
But hand over the sword now, or I'll show no mercy.
*cough*...
No.
Her wound reopens, and the young Anasa girl holds the sword in front of her chest with her left hand, throwing her chakram with her right, barely deflecting the enemy's attack.
She falls back step by step, as her arms feel heavier and heavier.
It just occurred to me that even if I let you go, you still need a way out.
How did you know I would be here?
I grew up here.
I was a mischievous boy who tried to sneak out of the city through the sand sluices, and was duly punished by my father for doing so.
But only someone as stubborn as you would dare take this way.
You still want to stop me.
Tell me, did you come to Yumen just for this sword?
Yes.
And you wish to present the sword as an offering?
Yes.
And after that, will you return it?
Well, I...
Yes, I will.
That eccentric from the clinic promised me earlier that if you broke your word, he would bring you back himself.
But having said all that, retrieving the sword was, and still is, my duty.
So while I have no qualms about taking you at your word, just this once, I will get that sword back, even if I have to jump into the sand sluice myself.
'Yi nuo qian jin.' You may not know the idiom, literally 'a promise worth a thousand gold.' Break your word, and I will drag you back here, personally.
...Alright.
You're wasting your effort. Hurt as she is, she'll die when she jumps down there.
Three quarters past the Hour of Yu... the turbines should have slowed down by now, there's no time to waste.
Get going!
The young Candleholder turns around to face his powerful foe, and a light gleams from his sword unsheathing.
The young Anasa girl hugs the sword tightly in her arms, and leaps down into the churning sand.
The observatory has recalculated its results according to the information we have just received.
The Catastrophe has manifested as a sandstorm, and its scale is about three times larger than the one that struck the city four years ago.
The prior misinformation has already cost us too much time, so the situation is not optimistic...
Zuo Xuanliao looks at the box in his hands, the surface clearly worn down by gravel and sand.
A few days ago, was there another person looking at this box just as he is now? How did he feel?
If there was a mirror in his military tent, Zuo Xuanliao would have noticed the strands of silver hair on his scalp multiplying over the past few days.
Let's get to the point.
Presently, Yumen is in the middle of the Catastrophe's trajectory, and won't be able to avoid it, even if we change course.
We have but one method available to us. We can split Yumen up into blocks and disperse in different directions to evade the Catastrophe.
How much time would that cost us?
Reconnecting after separation, and returning to full speed, will probably take about six months.
There's no time.
It is the only feasible way to ensure the city's safety.
It is not the city's safety we want to secure.
The General lingers in his tent, the night outside his window still seemingly serene. The unseen danger lurks far away, but he knows it is still out there.
He has seen these sights for decades, but he has never loathed the vast desert as much as he does right now.
If we can only ensure the safety of our people...
General, you mean to...?
Prepare the front for direct Catastrophe impact, and evacuate all civilians from the east side of the core city to the west for resettlement.
Activate all defensive fortifications, and station Imperial Astronomical Bureau Casters on the city walls to resist the aftershocks.
How long will repairs take, after the fact?
General Zuo, the risk is too great...
How long will they take?
Repairing the front walls and some of the city's infrastructure will take... by a conservative estimate, three months.
Sure...
Time is of the essence, so I urge the General to make a decision as soon as possible.
Mr. Liang?
Have you come to shackle me, to ensure I don't act on my own?
I'm here to relay the Grand Tutor's opinion—to trust in General Zuo's judgement.
Very well.
Prepare to confront the Catastrophe.
Hey, my bag! Don't push!
There's plenty of time, folks. Don't panic, get in line, careful not to step on each other.
Mama...
I'm scared...
Don't be scared, just hold my hand and stay close.
Where are we going?
There's a big storm coming.
So we're going to a safe place where we won't be blown away.
Massive shadows begin rising from outside the walls, blocking out the sun.
The Four Guardians of Yumen, retractable external mobile barriers known as the Wind Aegis, designed by Tumu Tianshi and built by the military's Department of Smelting and Fabrication.
For centuries, Yumen has served as Yan's border bulwark, and the Wind Aegis have been her shield, protecting her from the sandstorms, winter winds and Originium storms of the great desert.
The Three Winds shall not pass as long as the Four Guardians stand.
Wow, it's so tall...
I've never seen the Wind Aegis deploy in all my three years in Yumen.
Nothing to gape at.
Yumen's walls are tall, and fully equipped with Originium defenses. Mundane Catastrophes rarely require the Wind Aegis to be deployed, but it's not entirely unheard of.
However, this is the first time that we've had to move half the city's population, as far as I know...
So, er, wh-what is the class of this Catastrophe? So big not even the Wind Aegis could hold it back?
The Wind Aegis lock into place, steel plates interlocked so tightly not even the wind can get through.
Long lines of civilians move away from the walls in the shadows that the Wind Aegis cast. Perhaps comforted by the sturdy presence of the barriers, the crowd calms down after some initial chaos.
The poplars that grow on the borderlands have grown used to the wind and sand.
What's the matter? Scared?
Man your post. Don't think about anything else.
Keep your eyes open. The Shanhaizhong are still in the city. Anything can happen with so many people moving about.
Protect the commoners.
You're still here?
They've been broadcasting it all day. Yumen is about to run into a Catastrophe. Those on the east side need to move to shelters on the west side before noon.
I know, I know. The Catastrophe cloud isn't here yet. I need to pack my things...
Here it is.
I could lose the tables, the chairs, even the building, but not this recipe book.
Say, you could come with me to Lungmen, if you lost the inn.
Don't say that!
Sorry, yeah. That's bad luck.
I just figured your culinary skills would do well in Lungmen too. I've had the chance to see you work up close these few days, after all.
Nah, can't have that.
Where would the people of Yumen get my special sauteed meat if I left?
Are you a native of Yumen?
No.
I was born in the heartlands and went to Lungmen to seek my fortune. I came over when the cities linked, wanting to see what things were like in the north.
Then I lost my wallet and started working at this inn to make ends meet. Now I've been the innkeeper for years.
The building doesn't matter. Houses can be rebuilt.
Absolutely.
Well, I'll help you back up.
The winds are calm.
The dark, ominous mass of air is clearly visible, even from a distance, darkening part of the sky.
The Catastrophe does not seem to have moved, but the temperature is rising.
It is as though it is waiting for a gust of wind, before lashing out at the city walls.
Phew...
Come to watch my brother's duel, General?
Catastrophe preparations are already done. The Grandmaster wants to see whether that one is worth his trust before the Catastrophe strikes. I can't miss this.
Still, it seems absurd to determine the sword's ownership in this way.
You've been at my brother's side for decades. You would not trust his choice as a friend?
...Friend.
Hm.
We have fought side-by-side, but we have never been brothers. Neither sword nor spear, nor gun nor cannon can harm your kind. How could you truly understand the camaraderie of man?
You remain outsiders to it all.
'Outsiders'?
You could say that about me, but it seems unfair to apply it to my brother.
Man and beast are different. This has never changed.
You know how the imperial court views your kind.
An alliance can only endure when the two sides are equals. One should not wield power beyond one's control.
I'm not in a position to trust him unconditionally, for I'm not his equal.
I'm surprised you would be so frank.
But perhaps you underestimate yourself...
Zuo Xuanliao gazes wordlessly at the great desert beyond. The two shadows there are like two drops of ink on a great scroll.
Heaven and earth are silent.
It all feels like it happened yesterday.
We partook in more than just war. We hunted, drank, and sang together. Yumen is still Yumen. Why is it that you have worries enough to turn your hair white?
Does the sight of those two remind you of those golden days?
You say that man and beast are different, but look, a man has chosen to challenge this great barrier. Does your heart not race at a clash like that?
Loosen that brow of yours.
Care for a drink? We could even make a wager.
Good, good.
You finally agreed to fight me.
Sorry for making you wait.
It's only been three years. I could wait longer, if you have unfinished business.
Three years ago...
Hold it.
That was just one exchange.
It was enough.
We traded three blows in that exchange. Someone could have been hurt, if we were using our full strength.
But you're holding back, and I can't use my full strength either.
That's no fun.
Sparring is about restraint...
Screw sparring, screw restraint! Let's have a proper fight!
All the kung fu under Heaven evolved in the struggle of life and death. You're not talking like the top-ranked fighter on the Yumen Arena Board of Fame.
You have a point. However...
Fine, I know, you have a job to do. You're not in a position to go all-out.
When are you quitting? We'll put this off until then.
Well, I have no idea when that might be.
Guesstimate it for me.
Could be three years, five, ten, maybe more.
Alright. I'll wait.
You're right here, after all. I'm not worried you'll run away.
There's always more to do. This is the right time and place for the fight that I owe you.
I should explain that I have my own reasons for doing this.
Oh?
There's a tradition of military commanders sparring before their troops prior to a campaign, in order to inspire the men.
Yumen is about to fight a Catastrophe. This bout between you and I will inspire the warriors of the city.
Suits me just fine, as long as you're not worried about losing face when I make you eat dirt in front of the whole city.
Haha...
It's been many years since someone challenged me like this.
It's been many years since I've seen someone worth challenging like you.
Ready to give me your all?
Hold nothing back.
Victory and defeat lie in the hands of man.
Life and death lie in the hands of Heaven.
Did they start yet?!
Hey, take it easy, you're injured.
I'm a trained kung fu fighter. I'm not so fragile.
There's plenty of time to rest later. Miss this fight, and I may never see one like it ever again!
It's not that big a deal...
The Feline is your dad, right? The good-for-nothing old man you were talking about?
I definitely see the resemblance. Like father, like daughter.
In what way?
The way you look before a fight.
Like you're gonna eat someone.
Hmph...
I hear the Grandmaster's skills are almost supernatural. He hasn't fought in public in years. Some even say that he invented half the kung fu under Heaven.
And I hear your dad defeated that evil woman last night.
Who do you think will win here?
Silly question. Of course you want your dad to win.
That's his business, not mine.
I just want to see for myself how big the gap between us is.
Mr. Liang...
I didn't think you would be interested in such violent business.
I ought to be here. As a friend.
I wonder if he achieved his goal of making a name for himself.
Look at the number of eyes on this fight. It'll be the stuff of legend, no matter who wins.
A lunatic must answer to himself before telling others who he is.
We must all answer to ourselves.
You...
I was looking for Waai Tinp—Err, Huai Tianpei before trouble found me.
I went back to my old detective tricks and found out a few things. I have a good idea of what you're planning.
There's probably a reason why trouble finds you.
A big brother must look after his siblings.
What did you hear?
Few know that the Grandmaster is Ling's eldest brother. Seems like it's being kept a secret.
True...
Retrieving the Grandmaster's sword is, of course, an urgent matter, but the bigger headache is what to do with it afterwards.
You don't want it at Yumen. Perhaps you speak for the Sui Regulator, perhaps it's your judgment as advisor to Yumen, but the Lord Exorcist won't let it go.
You needed someone who could take the sword, someone both sides can live with.
You just happen to have an oath-brother who is a kung fu master, and he just happens to be in Yumen.
It didn't take much effort to guide the girl to the clinic where Huai Tianpei worked, in order to ensure his involvement.
From Shangshu to Yumen, you always get your old friends to solve your problems. Isn't that a little lazy?
I don't have many friends.
Question for you.
When did you start thinking about getting Huai Tianpei involved?
Never mind, no need to tell me.
*sigh*...
No matter how many strings you pulled behind the scenes, it was always his lifelong wish to fight the best and become the best. He might even thank you for setting the stage, if he knew.
We are all doing what we wanted to, even if things often don't turn out the way we want.
After all these years, perhaps he's the one who's closest to his oldest dreams.
'Achieve one thing in life.' Easy for him to say...
Mr. Huai came, despite his injuries...
He would have come as long as there was a single breath left in him. He doesn't care if it's the last thing he does.

I know Mr. Huai came to Yumen to challenge the Grandmaster, and the Grandmaster accepted the challenge. The Grandmaster has spoken of it from time to time, and I can tell you that he's no less looking forward to this than Mr. Huai is.
All kung fu fighters thirst for a good opponent.
It's an obsession, a strange one. It's natural that you don't understand.
It's the same reason that you follow the Grandmaster.
You can actually be kind of astute at times, you know that?
I'll head down now.
It's two masters at work. Nothing in your records will come close to what you are about to see.
I'll try to be detailed.
Just keep your distance. You don't want to get hurt.
Of course.
Earlier that day...
Today's the day, isn't it?
Are you ready?
The veterans say Yumen has been through many crises in the past, but nothing on this scale.
Not even you know what's going to come of it, do you?
Things are more complicated than meets the eye.
From Jiangqi to Yumen, how many times has she seen danger, seen her life hang by a thread?
Yet she did not die in the wilderness, nor did she become like the Shanhaizhong.
What is it that has kept her going?
This city has treated me well. I should do something for it.
Before that, however, I must answer to myself.
You said that you would fight me whenever I felt up to it.
I did.
Strike me, if you're ready.
Qiubai takes a step back, focuses, and raises her sword.
She knows that he knows her every move like the back of his hand.
An eye for an eye.
Is there wisdom in the saying, or is it simply a result?
How many people have fed the fins by the river fort? Should their families seek revenge on her father?
How many people would seek revenge on her, if she killed this one in front of her for the sake of her own revenge?
The cycle of vengeance never ends.
Should it really be hate that keeps her going?
How long has she been thinking about that question? The five years that she has been around this man, seeing all that he saw?
No, longer than that.
Her sword has seen blood every step of the way from Jiangqi to Yumen. How would she judge those dead by it?
That is why she came to Yumen, for revenge, and to learn.
Exact what must be exacted, and discern what must be discerned.
The sword is pulled back abruptly.
It leaves her hand and falls into a nearby ditch, sending a pack of fowlbeasts scattering in all directions.
The violence of the move almost injures her, showing none of her usual grace in practice or battle.
Her expression, however, is one of satisfaction.
That will do.
The sword has been thrust. It couldn't have killed you.
...Correct.
I didn't let go of vengeance because I can't kill you, though.
It's just not worth my sword anymore.

lines.st: When did she find the answer?

lines.x: It seems you have made great strides in the past three years.
We...
We've said too much.
I just...
Chongyue stops mid-sentence.
His opponent is injured. A few more words would give him time to adjust his breathing.
But he soon realizes that such kindness would be an insult to his opponent.
There is no such thing as a truly fair fight.
Cast away irrelevant thoughts, and give all for victory. That is what 'fair' is.
Let's begin.
Huai Tianpei clutches his fist, feeling the lines etched into his palm.
They are the marks of forty years of kung fu practice, his pride, his very emblem.
He's waited three years for this fight, but the preparation for it began on his first day studying kung fu.
A martial artist always aspires to the top.
The wind is still. The world is silent.
I saw two children fight over a fruit in that blurry mist.
They were about the same age and size, but one was quicker and punched heavier, and he knocked his foe to the ground.
He gets the fruit.
To the victor goes the spoils.
I saw that hunt.
Humanity declared war on Them. Mortals brought down gods that they could not even fathom, at the price of millions of lives.
Victory or death.

lines.st: What is the meaning of kung fu?
Strength? Skill? An instrument of killing?
One man, no matter how strong, can never overcome an entire army or a battleship.
Is assassination and backstabbing a kind of skill? Why pick one's means of killing? Why study it as a school?
Searching for an answer before this obstacle, I forsook my power, sealed 'myself' into a sword, and built myself a human body.
How long has it been?
How long has it been since the last time I encountered an opponent against whom I could give my all?

lines.x: Round 8.
The Grandmaster takes one step back before his opponent's assault. Huai Tianpei's fists are exhausted by the time they reach the Grandmaster's palm.
(Flexes bones)
Huff...

lines.st: For forty years I have studied kung fu. I have never seen a mountain I could not overcome, never known defeat.
My first shifu told me he had nothing left to teach me after six months.
Each subsequent shifu that I studied under sent me away after a year, at most.
There are hundreds of kung fu schools under Heaven, but what use are so many moves, when you only have one pair of hands, one pair of feet?
I will practice my own kung fu.
Heavier. Faster. More precise.
But this man before me...

lines.x: Round 9...
Huai Tianpei strikes at the Grandmaster's flank, cutting off his retreat.
The Grandmaster stays still and strikes back with equal force, cutting off Huai Tianpei's attack.

lines.st: The outcome is already clear... who could take nine hits from this man?
If he is a rock, then he is not facing a bigger rock, but a deep pool.
A pool with many rocks at its bottom.
How can one defeat Kung Fu itself?
But he is still standing.
Huai Tianpei digs deep into the sand, focusing his mind and breath, breathing out, breathing in.
Then, he holds up his fists again.
Move a mountain to fill a lake. If the pond cannot be filled, it only means that the rock is not big enough.

lines.x: (Coughs blood)
I lost.
I lost?
Round... 10.
The Record Keeper's ink brush stops.
He does not know how to record this.
It could not be simpler. Punch, hit, separate. No forms, no variations, just force against force, like two children fighting over food.
The only difference is that they are fighting over victory.
He has never seen Grandmaster wince in pain. Until now.
Wind.
...The Grandmaster has won.
(Coughs blood)
Hahaha!
Hahahahaha!
Looks like you weren't concerned with victory or defeat.
How could I not be?!
You're the First Under Heaven.
But all it took was forty years for me to get the upper hand on you, once. Another three hundred and sixty years, and I'll beat you and become First Under Heaven!
True, you would likely surpass me, if you had another three hundred years.
But how can a mortal live that long?
It's true that some people have lived three centuries, using Originium Arts to extend their lifespans.
I doubt that would be possible for Mr. Huai, however.
You're the First Among Men. No need to wait three hundred years.
Grandmaster... Mr. Huai has fainted.
Take him back to the barracks. Treat his injuries.
(Cheering)
(Bangs on sheathes)
Liang Xun, that man is your friend?
Yes.
Do you think he can be entrusted with the sword?
In terms of skill and character, there is no other choice.
Very well.
What say you, Lord Exorcist?
Zuo Xuanliao says nothing. He gazes at the battlefield below. One lies on the ground, one is standing. The outcome has been decided.
But the soldiers continue to bang their swords on their sheathes, a tradition among Yumen soldiers when they watch a demonstration.
It was a rare sight, a life and death struggle on a great battlefield, under the raging winds and sand.
Even the sickly general feels his heart race.
How many times has he fought alongside him? Has he ever fought so hard against any other foe?
If there is one to whom he can entrust the sword, it should be...
The Catastrophe...
It's coming.
I'm back, Shifu.
Jieyun limps towards a small mound by the nomadic city's route.
The grave is new, with a few poplars on each side that she planted to mark the place. Fortunately, it is close to where she thought it would be; the city has not traveled far.
She bows down and places the sword at the grave.
I said I would bring the sword back. I did it.
I went to the forge, saw the blacksmith, and the old pagoda tree.
The city is very different from how I imagined.
Is that a place that can be called 'home?'
Why are people not afraid to live on a great, moving plate?
They're not afraid that a Catastrophe would destroy their houses, not afraid that they won't have food or water.
I don't understand.
I don't understand the people in the city, or you, who left the city.
I don't understand what kind of person he is.

lines.st: He is brave, strong, invincible.
He is foolish, pathetic, alienated.
You held his sword. You were close to him.

lines.x: The voice feels far away. Is someone speaking to her?
She suddenly wants to see what the sword is like.
I'm leaving tomorrow.
The borders have been peaceful lately. Things should be fine even if I'm away for a while.
Such a hurry?
Your condition... and that of some of the others, they can't wait.
Just because there's no doctor who can treat it doesn't mean there's no one in Yan. If there's no one in Yan, perhaps there's another in these vast lands...
I have a brother who may be able to do it.
Take care while I'm away.
You're not a doctor. Why don't you ask what the patient wants?
My days may be numbered, but I want to see other places with the ones I have left.
All I've seen in my years at Yumen is sand as far as the eye could see. I want to travel, while I can still ride a burdenbeast.
To see Shangshu's famous mountains, taste Jiangnan's famous fresh water chestnuts...
With someone, hopefully.
You're sick. You need to stay put...
What do you know about being sick? You've never been sick or injured, have you?
Not only that, we've known each other for so long, but I've never seen a crease appear on your face.
We're not the same.
I'll be leaving. Don't think too much. Get some rest...
Is this a dream?

lines.st: She draws the sword.

lines.x: The ancient sword leaves its sheath.
Its shape is somewhat unusual, but otherwise it is an ordinary sword, not even sharp.
Through the blade, she sees the clouds in the sky, and the shadow of the sun.

lines.st: So this is how you separated yourself from It.
So obvious it was hardly worth thinking about.
You are the one who became most like Man.

lines.x: The sliver of the ghost leaves quietly.
He has found the answer.
I must go.
I need to return the sword... and ask him face to face.
Why are you sleeping on the walls, Miss Ling?! A Catastrophe is coming. Get down and get to a shelter.
Leave this to us.
What's the situation?
The Four Guardians have been deployed, as have the sand filters and Originium scrubbers. They are Yumen's first and firmest line of defense.
Given the size of the cloud formation, however, the Four Guardians will not be able to completely shut the storm out. Dust and Originium particles that get through will form a secondary storm.
The scale would be equivalent to a small Catastrophe.
Following the evacuation, all the plates on the east side have been retracted to avoid impact, but the plates of the inner city are immobile and will be impacted by the secondary storm directly.
How many are you?
The Imperial Astronomical Bureau has twelve casters stationed at Yumen.
How are you deployed?
Six here, six in the inner city.
Are six enough to protect the inner city?
The Lord Exorcist has deemed both locations critical.
The Imperial Astronomical Bureau will do its best.
Go to the inner city. Leave this place to me.
The danger is the same. Go.
Last time I saw a scene like this was decades ago, when I first came to Shangshu.
Yumen doesn't have brews as refined as Husong and Guixing, but Liedaozi is good stuff. Heavens, look at the scene you're treating me to.
A toast to you too, Heaven.
Ack, there's no goblet for me to throw right now.
A crack of thunder, so close it makes everyone's heart skip a beat.
The dark clouds are above the city in an instant.
Thick masses of air pull the sky down, while the darting arc lightning looks almost alive.
Raindrops evaporate the moment they form. The air is so dry that the slightest contact could set something on fire.
Yumen serves as the flint.
A single sight as far as the eye could see. How overwhelming, how terrible, how magnificent!
Come!
The lantern lights. The poet stands up, and drinks her fill from her flask.
The sandstorm and thunder rage, as earth and heaven become one stage.
Throw a goblet to the setting sun, toast the tower with sand overrun.
All of Heaven and earth, in the flask.
Increasing output.
Keep it up! The dams must run at maximum power.

The sand sluices have never been asked to churn so much sand, even with Yumen at full speed. With the city stationary right now, running the sluice dams at full power without the normal offloads would result in very high wear...
No time to think about that!
If all the sand and Originium dust that the Wind Aegis block piles up in front, it's going to swallow Yumen's foundation, jamming the whole city.
Maybe you should worry about yourself right now.
What the?!
The sky is unrecognizable.
Mr. Jing? Why are you here? Everyone should have evacuated to the west side.
I came back.
Those Shanhaizhong wangbadan are using the chaos to sabotage our Catastrophe defenses and harass the people. We caught them in the act a few times.
That's a job for the Patrol Battalion.
Can the Patrol Battalion deal with the Catastrophe and the Shanhaizhong at the same time?
Why do we carry swords, if all we do is hide at a time like this?
Hey, I help.
You're a tourist. There's no need to...
Yan saying, repay drop of water with river.
You buy me many drink. I repay.
I like your spirit. Alright, hop on.
But...
What's most important to Zuo Xuanliao? Protecting Yumen.
Catch.
Freshly brewed Liedaozi! The old man said to give you guys a treat.
Mr. Meng was...
No need for any of that.
All we need to know is that he died at the hands of these wangbadan.
Pah, these Yumenites didn't forget to take all their valuables, even when they're fleeing for their lives.
But there are a few jewelry shops on this street. They can't very well have taken all of it...
You just can't pass up a little looting, can you?
The other team has gone to close the sand sluices. Don't forget the mission.
I would never.
But they're ready for us.
A bunch of ruffians who haven't had a real fight in twenty years, satisfying themselves with fighting in the ring for show.
Useless rabble.
Here they come.
An impressive-sounding name won't change the fact that they're just a bunch of thieves and bandits.
Useless rabble.
They're spreading out and trying to cause as much chaos as possible. Don't let them get away!
I need a group to cut off Kangqing Street! It leads to the west side where the civilians are!
I go!
We'll have a drink and kill some bad guys.
Why does the trip back feel so far...?
The wind and sand are getting...
Found you... good thing your footprints weren't completely wiped away.
You weren't lying about planning to come back to Yumen.
Why are you here?
To get the sword back.
And to make sure you're safe.
Why are you hurt as well?
No time to talk about that. We need to get back to the city!
You were lucky to survive the jump down the sand sluice. Your injuries will take a long time to heal, though.
Here, hold on to me.
What is it?
I must be seeing things. There couldn't be anyone else out here at a time like this.
Let's go.
Yumen has come to a full stop, slightly off the expected location.
The Catastrophe has reached the eastern side of Yumen. Unable to hail Lord Wei at this time.
We're climbing.

lines.st: Though the path may sever, let not thy spirit falter.
Spring chill presses armor, autumn frost dapples Yumen's barrier.
That long dream, laments ere and now.

lines.x: Just look at Miss Ling.
The excitement is rubbing off on this rat.
They want to close the sand sluices.
They know how important it is right now... do the Shanhaizhong have people with engineering expertise?
(Sneers)
You can jump now, or I can throw your corpses down after I kill you.
Miss Qiu?
I heard Zuo Le came here, so I thought I would help out as well. Looks like I came at the right time.
You?
Shanhaizhong once sent an invitation to the river fort of Jiangqi, but we didn't get a response.
How ironic that you've chosen to take the side of the Yanese army.
You're well-informed.
Of course. We pay attention to those who walk our path.
Cut the crap.
Have you forgotten that you came to Yumen for revenge?
Good question. Unfortunately, I've already found the answer.
She raises her sword again.
This time, there is no need for her to pull back.
The man from Sargon swings at the Shanhaizhong member trying to attack the soldiers, forcing him back.
His Yanese is mediocre, but his attack is clean, powerful, pragmatic.
Nice!
So you weren't exaggerating back at the inn. You've got a couple of tricks up your sleeve.
No couple of trick, only one trick.
Grandmaster taught me when he came Sargon, but only one move...
They're still not backing off, even after losing so many. Are they not afraid to die?
What happen?
It's the sound of the storm impacting the Wind Aegis. Be careful.
This is a bigger Catastrophe than any Yumen has encountered before.
I don't even want to think about the Imperial Astronomical Bureau Casters who are taking the full brunt of it.
The Catastrophe is raging. They have their hands full dealing with it. Don't waste time here.
Take these ones out and retreat to the evacuation shelters on the west side.
Gotcha.
Where do you think you're going? We knew you were coming this way.
Round them up!
We'll leave the Catastrophe to the Imperial Astronomical Bureau Casters. These wangbadan are ours!
The sky has broken. Gravel and rock beyond reckoning clash, roll and rub against each other in the maelstrom, screeching like metal on metal.
The storm bears down on the tower, then hits a wall of Arts.
Hold your ground!
Form up!
That's the secondary storm after it passes through the Wind Aegis?
Mr. Lin! What are you doing here?
Lending a hand.
We can handle this. You should go back.
To where? How am I supposed to get my afternoon nap when there's a commotion like this out here?
The Originium scrubbers in the inner city can stop the particles from spreading, but the impact of the storm itself must be met with Originium Arts.
One more warm body.
Lin steps forward.
He has already taken off his coat.
I've manipulated sand for decades. I might as well find a coffin to sleep in now, if I can't make myself useful at a time like this.
Or are my little tricks just a burden to the great Tianshis?
...Just be careful, sir.
Make way for Mr. Lin!
That's right.
Dad! I've been looking for you...
Perfect timing, Yühsia.
You're hurt!
Will that get in the way of anything?
Watch. You still have much to learn when it comes to Arts.
The storm converges again in mid-air and approaches once again. Dirt and rocks spin chaotically on the outer edge, while the air has been so compressed at the center that sparks are flying.
The heavens roar, then silence.
A wall of sand rises, filling every gap in the wall of Arts, pushing it slowly but steadily forward. One meter, three meters... ten meters.
Lin stands at the center of the casters, a great tree that budges for no storm.
I've never seen Arts like this before...
To achieve such stability over such a large area...
It's coming again!
Lin Yühsia has never seen the full extent of her father's Arts. It has never been needed for his enemies.
The shadow of Lungmen leads a regular life.
Taking 'walks' through the streets, enjoying a bowl of finball soup, visiting people, living and dead.
Most of the time, he is smiling, nonchalant. There are few things that make him bare his fangs.
He looks much smaller without his coat. Yühsia can see his arms begin to tremble, like thin branches that could be broken by a stiff wind at any time.
Help Mr. Lin down.
He has taken the brunt of four waves of Originium storms. He can take no more.
(Coughs blood)
Dad!
*pant*...
*pant*...
'I just hope this will be resolved quickly without delaying your trip to Victoria.'
'It's not your own choice if you haven't seen another path.'
Those are the kind of things that Dad always said.
Lin Yühsia did not want her father to set her path. Perhaps she has not made a decision yet.
But Dad is already old. He was not joking when he talked about retiring.
When the old fall, it is time for the young to take their place.
The logic is illogical, yet immovable.
Yühsia steps forward and takes her father's place, shielding him behind her.
Miss Lin...
Don't speak. You're all injured. Adjust your breathing.
Here it comes.
Keep your eyes forward!
Crack.
A thick layer of gravel has formed on the streets.
Most of the south side has been spared the effects of the Catastrophe, but at the peak of the storm, there was nowhere in Yumen where the sun could be seen.
Crack.
The lock is broken.
The furnace has been toppled, racks scattered everywhere, the yard littered with broken bricks and tiles.
The master of the house will never return to clean this up.
Yet the tree still stands.
Grandmaster.
Thank you for coming here to the Sword Forge.
The sword has been found, but she insists on returning it to you personally.
I need to talk to you.
Go ahead.
It's been more than a decade since I was last here.
It's like coming home, even though we're in the same city.
About Mr. Meng...
There's no need to say anything.
He knew it was wrong to put the whole city in danger. I reckon he had planned to atone for it from the beginning.
Still, my ill-considered actions have alienated the jianghu heroes of Yumen.
I will speak to my father and accept any discipline, once this crisis has passed.
And I will indemnify any losses suffered by the Sword Forge, as well as the costs of any repairs.
Lord Exorcist is fair and just. He will give them a proper answer.
However...
The events that transpired were sudden, and some misunderstanding is understandable. As for whether you lost your calm and stepped out of bounds... I will let you be your own judge.
I did what had to be done. My conscience is clear.
—Mm.
I'm sure you understand that the Shanhaizhong are even more dangerous to Yan than the Feranmuts.
The Feranmuts may be powerful, but we won the hunt a thousand years ago. Yan is even stronger now. There is nothing to fear.
True. Times have changed.
What is truly frightening is the shadow that had been cast on people's hearts since time primordial.
Heaven and earth, the moon and stars, wind and rain, the changing of the seasons... it is unfathomable, beyond our reckoning.
The Feranmuts were the same to the people of the time.
Ignorance breeds fear, which becomes twisted into religion. To those madmen, we are the ones we are the outsiders. We are the traitors.
Religion is simply another kind of obsession.
The Shanhaizhong's activities never waned, even after the Feranmuts faded from the public eye.
In the past centuries, they have absorbed bandits, pirates, robbers, and other sorts of villains. They use the Feranmuts as a shadow to conceal their activities.
Sui Regulator records indicate that the Shanhaizhong have done unmeasurable harm in the name of the Feranmuts.
The scale of their deeds is so widespread that complete elimination is impossible, and the vileness of their acts horrifying.
They have started to reach out to the proxies of your kin. They were thankfully foiled by the timely intervention of the Sui Regulator.
I doubt my siblings want anything to do with such 'disciples.'
However, the Sui's true form stirs from slumber, Yumen returns to its motherland, and the Shanhaizhong have infiltrated the city. It is a complex web, too complex to be a coincidence.
Do you mean...?
All under Heaven is the offender's Go board. The Shanhaizhong may simply be a piece in his game.
That's a serious accusation. Is it the Sui Regulator's official stance?
Or perhaps, the Shanhaizhong have already made contact with the Sui.
Therefore, the Sui Regulator sees this as the greatest Feranmut threat that Yan has faced in its history.
Yes.

Tracking the sword, dealing with the criminals, hunting the Shanhaizhong... you are the youngest Candleholder of the Sui Regulator, and son of the Lord Exorcist of Yumen. In this matter, your burden has no equal.
Yes.
In a world of chaos, decisions of life and death must be swift and forceful as thunder.
Err on the side of going too far.
Better to be too harsh, than too lenient.
That is what you think.
...Yes.
There is a young Zuo Xuanliao in you.
However, you seem to have forgotten Zuo Xuanliao's last instructions when he ordered you to do three things in three days.
Keep this secret, and do not disturb the people.
What was the ultimate goal of the imperial court when the Sui Regulator was established to deal with Feranmut matters?
Why has Yumen returned to the motherland, its survival at stake?
It's for the people in this city right now. The people whom you brought back to the camp.
...For all the people.
Never forget what all you do is for.
Maybe you were right in this instance, but a position of authority and holding the power to make decisions also means holding the power to kill or harm. Some mistakes can never be undone.
Though it shouldn't have been me to tell you all this.
Thank you for your guidance, Grandmaster.
The Catastrophe rages on. Go and help.
Boss, everybody, get in here.
This out-of-the-way alley is a blind spot in the Patrol Battalion's search routes. We're safe here for now.
Finally, a breather... is this all the men we have left?
We lost the rest on the street.
The Yumen soldiers moved much faster than expected, setting up watch around Catastrophe defenses and key roads in a very short time.
And those guards and brawlers who appeared out of nowhere. Cao, they were tough...
What about the Di beast we sent out?
None returned.
The other groups are probably no better off. If they were captured alive...
A rabble of greedy crooks who were recruited to not leave a trail to begin with. They won't give away anything important.
We've done what we came for. Let's go while we still have the Catastrophe, before Yumen can cut off our escape.
By the way... where's the Master?
Where is she at this crucial time?
She's...
You...
I took your sword. Now I'm giving it back.
Since you've returned it, I'll say you borrowed it.
I want to ask you a few questions. I'll only give it back after you've answered.
I know you're much stronger than I am, but don't try to take it by force.
Very well. Ask.
Why don't you look anxious at all? Don't you care about the sword?
A worldly possession. One that cannot be easily discarded.
I wouldn't say I don't care.
Either you care about it or not.
Shifu was right. The way that you beat around the bush can be annoying.
The one who said that...
Is the sword called 'Chongyue?'
'Shuo.'
Chongyue is my name.
Shifu often talked about this sword.
But maybe it's not just the sword that she wanted to see.
For the last couple of days, I've been thinking, is this Shifu's home?
Shifu taught us how to fight, taught us knowledge from the nomadic cities.
How to build sturdier houses to stand against the sandstorms, and how to listen to the pulse of the land in order to plant crops and dig for water, even in the barrens.
The nomadic city that Shifu talked about was tall and strong. I saw how the people turned back even an all-consuming Catastrophe, when they stood together with each other.
Everyone should have a place to call home.
If home lets us rest without fear of disaster or having to chase the seasons...
Then why did Shifu leave twenty years ago?
The man does not answer.
She was very ill, but she taught you kung fu?
We Anasa are gifted with strong bodies. Shifu said that when one has a home, one must learn to protect it.
We learned quickly, so that Shifu would not have to demonstrate over and over again with her sickly body.
My people thought about taking her away from the barrens to get treatment.
Shifu always answered, 'But how draining would the journey be? I would rather see how the Liedaozi I taught you to brew is coming along.'
Did you ever find a doctor?
The man shakes his head.
Not in the places I looked. I was going to look elsewhere, when I received an urgent message from Yumen.
The Shanhaizhong were wreaking havoc in Yumen. They asked me to return as soon as possible.
So Shifu's illness could not have been treated, even if she never left.
...Yes.
Shifu said that the cycle of life and death is something everyone goes through.
A little longer or shorter doesn't change anything.
What really separates us is the 'age' between life and death, and even more cruel 'illness.'
What can be seen but cannot be helped merely makes one troubled.
The first batch of Liedaozi was finished just before Shifu passed away. She drank it with us and heard us sing a song with no name.
It was an old song that the Anasa sang for our dead kin to put their souls to rest. Shifu asked what point there is in singing a song for her, if she can't hear it?
'Let me hear it while I still can.'
Good. She didn't die alone.
I was eating at the inn yesterday and saw that people were preparing to see you off.
Did you...?
Are you leaving?
It's been more than a hundred years. It's time to go.
You've been here so long. Isn't this your home?
Many people's homes are somewhere else in Yan, but they stay here to protect this city.
But where it's going, I can't go with it.
Would you have gone to look for Shifu, if she was still alive?
The spring comes and goes, leaving the autumn heart despondent.
Such is mortality.
You are like the Anasa.
Anasa. Those without roots.
(In ancient Sarkaz) ...Maybe.
The words and intonation are indistinguishable from a native.
The Anasa girl knows, however, that he is not one of her kind.
He has simply gone to many places and met many people.
Forget it. Here's your sword. I have nothing more to ask.
The man never answered any of the questions.
He neither answered, nor dodged them.
The Anasa suddenly understands how her shifu must have felt when she left.
I'm going home.
The Anasa girl falls to the ground.
She has passed out from sheer exhaustion.
Her injuries...
She pulled the sword...
The ancient sword leaves its sheath.
It looks no different, save for the vestige of an old friend, almost gone now.
The medium is the jade on her forehead...
She used to wear it, saying that it calmed her mind and warded off evil...
I see.
A move decades in the making.
But a poor one, if the sword was the only goal.
...The one in the city?
He had his eye on one corner of the board, ignoring the other.
Hah.
So this is what it is...
This is how you have fallen...
How amusing.
I want to see how you swallow it...
The shame.
'Sui?'
First the Lord Exorcist's tent, now the central room of Yumen's inner city. Have you never been taught that it is rude to come uninvited?
You?
You got your sword back.
Just now.
Looks like he has discovered your secret.
So you have met my brother...
You separate that part that belonged to It and sealed it away, while he wants to take its place.
The attitude with which the two of you treat It, what you are doing, is bold, and interesting.
But ultimately foolish and pointless.
Perhaps no more than what you are doing right now.
You're not here for the Catastrophe, for the city, or for what I hold in my hands right now.
You made all this mess just to ask for directions.
You wanted to know where the Sui is.
How dare you compare those things to It?
You are slow on the uptake.
It slipped my mind as to who would be so interested in It, other than the imperial court and my siblings.
You have forgotten what you used to be.

lines.st: Only a Feranmut would seek out another.

lines.x: Long time.
No see.
An utterance with a million connotations behind it.
Poignant lament, cold sarcasm, or seething anger, a thousand emotions exploded at once, then dissipated.
They dig wounds in the depths of memories immemorial.
In time beyond reckoning, It and It, They, engaged in dialogue form afar as such.

lines.st: I came back to this land to settle our debts.
But what right does a fragment sealed in a trinket have to engage in dialogue with me?
I want to see you as who you truly are.

lines.x: There's something I'm curious about.
You fled after the hunt, not to be heard from all these years. Why have you chosen to return to Yan at this time?
It was my brother who awakened you...
As for the Shanhaizhong that appeared with you...
They? Hah.
Just made some company on the way.
I encountered them in the desert. I shared the forming Catastrophe that I saw, and made a little show of power.
They were surprised that someone could know the Feranmuts so well. They were convinced that I was a herald of the Feranmut.
Some are pretentious, some sincere, but all strive to prove their faith to me, worshipping bits and pieces that they claim to have come from the Feranmuts.
They curse their own kind and imagine overthrowing all order. They look down on mankind, utter meaningless words, and implore me to communicate their faith.
Little do they know that the object of their faith itself stands before them.

If your encounter with the Shanhaizhong was by chance, and you had intended to sneak into the central room all along, in order to lock on to the Sui's location through Yumen's navigational coordinates...
Why are the Shanhaizhong here?
To destroy Yumen? Not very likely.
Until they met you, they didn't know there would be a timely Catastrophe in Yumen's path.
I'm not interested in mankind's petty machinations.
I just needed some hands to cause chaos.
In human terms, you manipulated your followers.
And?
To become infected with hysteria and start thinking about one's own existence, even denying it.
Only slightly more amusing than those who remain foolish, from their first moments to their last.
That is to say, not very amusing.
What would they think, if they knew their god thought so little of them?
Since when have we needed the faith of such puny beings?
Except for fools like the Sui.
Leaving so soon?
I spared some time for you only because you are Its fragment.
I've already wasted too much time.
I should have realized the other day when we fought. The scent of flowers and the sight of spring were too real to be achieved by Originium Arts.
Sui Regulator records speak of a beast by the name of Ya, who cuts away the seasons and holds them at its bosom.
You have Its memory, yet you dare call me in the human way!
What a disciple name, just like 'Sui!'
Cuts away the seasons?
Humans measure time in such silly ways.
It is no illusion. An illusion cannot be so real.
It ceases to exist in some other fleeting time.
It exists in this never-ending present.
Time has stopped.
Eight thousand years of spring, eight thousand years of autumn.
A maze that has no ending.
Time has not stopped.
A lone boat on a river, each one standing on one end.
A ripple spreads from the end of the boat.
You're trying to make me stay?
Yes.
You demonstrate incredible mastery of space, but I must make you stay.
Even if I can't.
I would have left Yumen by now, if things had gone according to plan.
For you, neither Catastrophe nor Yumen can compare in importance to the Sui.
For me, however, there is nothing that I care less about than the Sui.
For you, all that is happening outside is a fleeting cloud.
For me, it is the damaged walls, broken houses, suffering soldiers and civilians.
And... one old friend who died with regret.
The Catastrophe has not passed. They do not even know that you exist.
Someone must make a stand for Yumen.
I guess there's no one but me to do it.
The second long journey through the vast void.
Awakening at the end of the journey—
Regret. Shame. Anger. Disorientation.
But even more so, confusion.
You want me to stay? Then answer my question for It!
I came so far to find you.
Did you know that those ants have dug through mountains and filled in rivers, calling the scars they leave upon the land 'cities?'
Is this frail and sick land still the cosmos that we had once lived and frolicked in?
Did you know that the marks They left before They died remain on this despicable city they call Yumen?
The ants have not repaired it for a thousand years, calling it part of the glorious journey towards civilization.
This is the city that your fragment has sworn to protect?
It is It. I am I.
(Heaven's repressed anger)
The boat continues to sail.
The plants on the banks grow shorter, pressed down by the snow.
Do you remember how our consciousnesses journeyed the sleepless void and became the first living beings here?
Do you remember how we watched the sun rise and set, giving order to time and objects, and Heaven and earth took shape?

Do you remember that eye which broke the heavens? We watched and clashed in the cosmos, the rivers turned red, the mountains leveled, the bodies piled up until they reached the clouds, becoming new mountains when they rotted away...
Such a magnificent thing a true war is!
(Heaven's repressed anger)
The boat continues to sail upon a river without end.
The snow grows thick. Each question echoes like an avalanche.
It is white up above and down below. The skies are so low that one only has to reach out to touch them.
Why would one who has seen such an era care about the ants between its claws, and the dust that they stir up?
To deceive humanity, you meddled with cloud and rain, sent preposterous 'blessings' from the heavens, helped the so-called True Lung triumph. How utterly meaningless...
Why do you answer them again after a thousand years? Harass, provoke, hunt and kill your own kind?!
Compared to their ancestors, puny but brave, these ants are feeble, despicable, filthy. Why do you want to rule over them?!
Why do you want to become their god?!
(Heaven's repressed anger)
Since time immemorial, has our kind ever seen the shame that you have put yourself to?!
Answer me, my... my kin!
You're angry.
In all these millennia of slumber, It has ever been like you are now...
Swallowing the shame, chewing upon betrayal, seeking answers.
That is why fragments like us were born.
But we are not It.
I cannot answer your question.
I cannot understand your fury, just as you cannot understand mine.
Fury?
You will stand for humanity? Here?
Draw the sword, and maybe you can.
I'm thinking how much you treasure this dimension.
Born in spring, prosper in summer, wilt in autumn, fade away in winter. Cut away the seasons, and hold it at one's bosom.
I could imagine how you hurriedly cut away this little pocket and took it with you when you fled Yan a thousand years ago.
How you hid in a remote corner of the world, flipping through the pages again and again, licking your wounds before a snippet that will never fade.
Since when have the Feranmuts become so sentimental?
How is it different from those feeble, despicable and filthy humans you speak of?
Eight thousand years of spring, eight thousand years of autumn. The snow never stops falling. This little corner will ever be itself.
This is how you measure time...
How silly.
Days, months, years.
Winter turns to spring, then to winter again.
Time can be divided, no matter how long.
One mind, one will, one move. From equinox to solstice, to the next equinox.
Humanity has always been so, breaking down life into tiny constituents, and perceiving everything within those constituents.
The boat stops.
A firm fist connects with Ya's chest.
There is no extraneous motion, only the utmost simplicity.
The ice and snow melt into a single point on the river. Ya falls from the boat...
And hits the cold, hard ground.
This is what you learned after you forsook Its power...
You are not It...
The man does not speak. He watches the 'human' before him, in its last throes.
All the living things of the world do not look so different on the brink of death.
Why... haven't you killed me?
No point. You can reshape your proxy's body at will.
Leave Yumen.
We'll meet again.
*cough*...
Sir, the initial damage report is in.
Casualties?
All civilians on the east side were safely evacuated to the west side.

Half of the Imperial Astronomical Bureau Casters deployed at the inner city to deal with the secondary storm were injured. Also on scene were Mr. Lin of Lungmen and his daughter, Envoy Lin... Mr. Lin suffered serious injuries.
H-He is being treated at the army hospital.
All of Yumen owes Mr. Lin a great debt. Spare neither money nor manpower.
Yes, sir.
Damage to buildings?
The west side was unaffected. A number of buildings were damaged by the storm in the east and north sides.
The east side took the brunt of the storm.
Two of the Four Guardians were damaged beyond economical repair and have been detached by the Tianshi.
Part of the foundation wall has been severely warped. Emergency repairs are in progress.
About 60% of all city defenses have been damaged.
The Tianshis' assessment?
Initial estimates are that it will take at least two months before Yumen can move again, even with full effort devoted to the repairs.
Two months...
Slightly better than pre-Catastrophe estimates.
Here are my orders.
The entire army will remain alert for potential after-storms.
Continue to assess damage and treat the injured. Erect emergency shelters to house the evacuated population.
Yes, sir.
And the Shanhaizhong?
They attempted to cause chaos by attacking city defenses and harassing the people. Most have been brought to justice by the Patrol Battalion's prompt intervention, assisted by civilian volunteers.
Only a few managed to escape through gaps in the damaged walls.
...I see.
However, we have not found the Shanhaizhong leader responsible for the assassination attempt at the barracks. Search efforts are underway.
She did not show herself?
Strange, given her ability...
What of the Grandmaster?
The Grandmaster visited the Sword Forge. He has not been seen since.
Why did you give me this sword?
I want you to keep it safe... consider it a request.
So this was the sword that all the fuss was about.
What is it, anyway?
It is too many things.
An artifact holding one of the twelve fragments of the Sui, witness to a relationship of love and hate, an old antique sword.
A keepsake for our next fight.
Just don't let anyone steal it again, or pawn it for money.
Right...
Sounds important.
Well, if it's too much trouble...
I'm just thinking... we don't really know each other that well.
I know your name is Huai Tianpei. You have two wise friends, and a talented daughter.
And you are the only one who has ever gotten the upper hand on me, even once.
Right.
I know you're the only one I couldn't beat, in my forty years of training.
I'll take the sword.
I'll find you after I've trained for another forty years.
Right.
I'll be waiting.
Excuse me, doctor.
How much does the man who worked at your clinic owe you? I'll pay it.
Nah, he worked that off ages ago.
It was just a convenient excuse to take him in.
Better for him to be in here mending people's bones, than out there breaking them.
Thank you... for everything.
Are you his daughter?
Yes...
Just found it hard to believe he had a daughter so polite and well-mannered. No offense, of course.
Still, it takes some getting used to, not having him around.
How am I going to clean up all this sand by myself?
I-I'll help!
Left without a word? Again?
Yeah.
Some men never change.
I'm not so anxious to find him this time around, though.
Looks like you've made your own plans.
I have my job at the agency, and school. And kung fu, of course.
He'll get more than just a kick in the chin the next time I see him.
Orders from above. We have our next mission. We'll go once we've rested and recovered.
Too bad Yumen survived the Catastrophe, while we took heavy casualties.
(Sneers)
The letter burns in the masked man's hand, quickly turning to ash, making a tiny corner of gold even more conspicuous.
Paint from a special seal that only top imperial mandarins may use. Only five people have access to it.
The man rubs the seal in his hand, crushing it into tiny pieces that fade into the sand.
Our mission was to cause a commotion that would catch the imperial court's attention. We still have a use for Yumen. It will not do to lose it.
We move now.
We're not waiting for Master? We could hardly have done this without her.
It's the Di beast... Master is back!
Careful...
Who in Yumen could have injured you like this?!
A 'Man.'
What else did you hear?
(Eerie roar)
How long?
(Eerie roar)
Two months?
...That's enough.
I know where It is.
What do you... what are you...?
Master, I'm afraid we must part here.
Suit yourself.
Just don't get in my way.
There is always an end to the path, no matter how long.
I have felt it since my awakening, growing ever clearer. What they call 'Mortality.'
The wounds from that hunt will never fully heal.
I hope you have awakened.
I have little time left. I have few kin left.
Let us settle this.
How is Mr. Lin doing?
The doctor says he'll be fine.
It's just that his old injuries and overuse of Arts have taken a toll on him.
Dad is getting old, after all.
Sometimes I forget how long Mr. Lin has served Lungmen.
Dad is not like Chief Wei. As the symbol of Lungmen, Chief Wei always stays in the public eye.
But Lungmen doesn't exactly want everyone remembering that there is a Rat King of Downtown.
I heard Wei Yenwu dragged you into this.
It became my business when I chose to accept it, of my own accord.
And it all turned out fine, thanks to a certain someone.
Tsk.
This incident also helped me answer a couple of questions.
Are you going to Victoria? We could travel together.
No, my plans have changed.
I'm going back to Lungmen.
What do you mean by 'going back to Lungmen?'
I'm staying.
Throwing off your burdens sounds nice, but whatever you throw off, somebody else has to pick up.
Whether one leaves or stays is up to oneself, but someone has to take care of Lungmen.
Mm...
I said that for me, but feel free to take it as a criticism, if you want.
Glad to see you still have the strength to joke.
The daughter of the Rat King doesn't have to be the Rat King, but it doesn't mean she can't be.
She could be a different sort of Rat King.
This time, I made my own choice.
There are slums in Lungmen, but there shouldn't be. It's a job that someone has to do, no matter how many years go by.
The times are changing, but no one should be left behind.
I believe that, when it's you saying it.
By the way...
What is it? Your turn to hem and haw?
Have you seen Wei Yenwu these past few days?
Are you leaving, Grandmaster?
Yes.
Once I've handed over my duties.
Speaking of which, I must thank you.
For what?
You chose to trust Yumen.
And you chose to trust my friends.
It's all water under the bridge.
We suffered some losses, but we should count ourselves fortunate that the situation remained under control.
More importantly, I saw how the people here remain united, even after so many years.
Yumen hasn't changed. Man will triumph over Heaven.
Now I can be at ease about leaving.
There's no telling when we'll meet again.
Take care, Grandmaster.
You too. Beware the intrigue of the court.
I will.
By the way, you said something about Ch'en's swordsmanship yesterday.
'Skillful, but not yet the peak of perfection.'
Yes, I remember.
Ch'en has talent, but she only just started learning the Chi Xiao style recently.
She has not mastered its true essence.
Ah, you wanted to show off your skills.
I said before that Cloud-splitting sword is not actually Chi Xiao's ultimate move.
His fingers are his sword.
A light breeze begins blowing out of nowhere, creating tiny ripples in the teacup.
The ultimate move of Chi Xiao, Heaven's Eye.
The form?
The eye of Heaven sees all and nothing.
The concept?
Only when the clouds are parted is Heaven's wrathful eye revealed.
One must free one's mind to learn the form.
The obstacle?
Let your heart guide the sword, never looking back.
Turn around, and the sword loses its sharpness, harming the user.
Farewell.
The breeze continues to blow, long after the chair is empty.
The man gazes into the ripples in the teacup, until a smile appears on his face.
I have never seen the likes of it.
Come in.
Grand Tutor.
You understand that entering Yumen without permission is enough to carry the suspicion of treason on its own?
Yes.
Lord Wei was attacked, and trapped in Yumen, while Yumen travels towards the capital...
We were concerned for Lord Wei's safety. The urgency of the situation did not allow for deliberation.
We have nothing but our lives to repay Lord Wei's kindness.
Did Wei Yenwu tell you to look for him in Yumen if he did not return in three days?
That is so.
I can tell you now that both the assassination attempt on Wei Yenwu and the crisis at Yumen were accidents. There is no need to worry.
I understand.
Where were you when the Catastrophe happened?
We operated in secret, eliminating insurgents stealthily.
At least you did one thing you were supposed to.
You may go.
We are not subject to the Grand Tutor's punishment?
As far as the Imperial Guard is concerned, all of you are already dead.
I never saw you today.
Yes, Grand Tutor.
You knew.
From the beginning.
And you never had any intention of hiding it from me.
Do you not reproach me for overstepping my boundaries?
The situation remained under control thanks to the young envoy that you brought from Lungmen.
The Grand Commandant will soon learn of this incident.
'Soon' is not the word I would use.
This is why I came to Yumen.
There is no consensus on how we should deal with the bestial Sui, whether we should prepare for war, or reconciliation. It is hard to see the forest when one is amongst the trees.
The minds of some of the parties concerned may no longer be on finding the right answer.
You wanted to help the Lord Exorcist eliminate the insurgents out of a sense of duty to Yan.
As long as you don't lose sight of it, you won't need me to tell you what to do.
I will remember your teachings.
You have always been a good student.
I remember when I taught you and your brother. You were always the quicker learner.
Are you injured?
Just a scratch.
The Tumu Tianshi say it was you who protected the sand sluices.
I let them get away, though.
By the way, I came to say goodbye.
...Alright.
I will find a place where this sword will realize its potential, even if you're not with me anymore.
I have no doubt you will succeed.
There is no more hatred between us, but I haven't given up on surpassing you.
I'll come and find you, when I feel ready.
Like I said, anytime you feel ready.
See you again.
No regrets?
What regrets?
Who will she seek if one day It wakes from Its dream, and you and I return to the void?
She managed to let go of her obsession with vengeance at her age. She will let another one go when I'm gone, and forgotten from her mind.
Then the sky is the limit for her on the path of kung fu.
Well, you are the Grandmaster. You're the authority on kung fu.
